message:
feat(scheduler): add NUMA-aware reactor shard placement with topology snapshots and deterministic binding manifests

Implement topology-aware shard placement for the ReactorMesh, enabling
deterministic NUMA-node-to-shard binding based on machine topology:

- Add ReactorTopologyCore and ReactorTopologySnapshot types for
  representing normalized machine topology from (core_id, numa_node) pairs
- Introduce ReactorPlacementFallbackReason enum with explicit codes for
  topology_unavailable, topology_empty, and single_numa_node scenarios
- Implement ReactorPlacementManifest with plan() that spreads shards across
  NUMA nodes in round-robin fashion, falling back gracefully when topology
  is unavailable or degenerate
- Add as_json() method for stable machine-readable placement manifest export
- Wire placement_manifest into ReactorMesh::new and expose via accessor
- Extend ReactorMeshConfig with optional topology field

Tests:
- Deterministic placement: identical topology produces identical bindings
- NUMA round-robin: 6 shards across 2 NUMA nodes yields [0,1,0,1,0,1] node
  assignment with correct core-within-node cycling
- Fallback recording: missing topology sets TopologyUnavailable reason
- JSON export: mesh exposes structured manifest with single_numa_node detection

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
